c74object.name: fluid.robustscale~
c74object.digest: Apply Robust Scaling to a FluidDataSet
c74object.description: Apply Robust Scaling to a DataSet based on statistics of the data such that each dimension has a median centred on 0 and a range of 1 from the low percentile to the high percentile.

discussion.p: For example, using the default values for low (25) and high, RobustScale will find the range between the 25th and 75th percentile for each dimension and then scale each dimension accordingly so the range between the 25th and 75th percentile the scaled data is 1. RobustScale also offsets each dimension so the median is centred on 0.

Because RobustScale is based on percentiles, it is less affected by extreme outliers than other scalers (such as Standardize and Normalize). This may help RobustScale position the majority of the data in a -1 to 1 -ish range better than other scalers.
Read more about fluid.robustscale~ on the learn platform.
fluid.robustscale~ is part of the Fluid Decomposition Toolkit of the FluCoMa project. For more explanations, learning material, and discussions on its musicianly uses, visit flucoma.org.

description.p: Name for this instance, so that it can be reused or referred to by other data objects. If none is given, a name will be generated

method.name: reset
method.digest: Return the object to its original state
method.description: Calling reset will change parameter values back to those the object was created with, i.e. any attribute values set in the box will be retained

method.name: refer
method.description: Changes the reference to the data in another named fluid.robustscale~ object. Changes to the data stored in any referenced will be shared by all other similarly named fluid.robustscale~ objects.

description.p: Compute the scaling factors from a fluid.dataset~ for later.

ul.li:
dataSet:
The fluid.dataset~ to determine the statistics of.

sourceDataSet:
The fluid.dataset~ to determine the statistics of and scale.

destDataSet:
The fluid.dataset~ to write the scaled data to.

description.p: Scale a fluid.dataset~ into another fluid.dataset~, using the learned statistics from the previous call to fit

ul.li:
destDataSet:
The fluid.dataset~ to write the scaled data to.

description.p: Scale a data point, using the learned statistics from the previous call to fit

ul.li:
sourceBuffer:
A buffer~ with a data point

destBuffer:
A buffer~ to write the scaled values to

description.p: Inverse scale a fluid.dataset~ into another fluid.dataset~: going from the range of the scaled data back to the range of the data that was used in the previous call to fit

ul.li:
sourceDataSet:
The fluid.dataset~ to inverse scale

destDataSet:
The fluid.dataset~ to write the scaled data to.

description.p: Inverse scale a data point, going from the range of the scaled data back to the range of the DataSet that was used for fit.

ul.li:
sourceBuffer:
A buffer~ with a data in the scaled range

destBuffer:
A buffer~ to write the un-scaled values in the original range to

description.p: The number of columns (dimensions) in this model or dataset / labeset
Resets the internal state of the model
The number of data points (entries / observations) in this model or dataset / labeset

method.name: load
method.description: Replace the internal state of the object from a dict. The message should take the form load dictionary <dictid>

method.name: dump
method.digest: Dump the state of this object from the dump outlet as a dict.

method.name: read
method.description: Replace the internal state of the object from a JSON file on disk. With no argument, a file dialog will appear to choose the file. It is always executed at the lowest priority on the main thread, using deferlow internally.

method.name: write
method.description: Save the internal state of the object to a JSON file on disk. With no argument, a file dialog will appear to choose the file. It is always executed at the lowest priority on the main thread, using deferlow internally.

description.p: The high percentile boundary. The default is 75.
The low percentile boundary. The default is 25.
Enable warnings to be issued whenever a parameter value is constrained (e.g. clipped)
